name: Hylas
description: Hylas was a young Argonaut, renowned for his exceptional beauty and his close bond with Heracles. He was the son of King Theiodamas of the Dryopes and the nymph Menodice (or, in some accounts, Thiodamante and the nymph Nais). Hylas joined Jason and the Argonauts on their quest for the Golden Fleece, serving as Heracles' armor-bearer and beloved companion. During a stop at Cius in Mysia, Hylas went to fetch water from a spring called Pegae. His beauty captivated the nymphs of the spring, who were so enamored with him that they dragged him down into the water, never to be seen again. Heracles, distraught at the loss of his beloved Hylas, searched frantically for him, delaying the Argonauts' journey and ultimately leading to Heracles abandoning the quest altogether. The story of Hylas serves as a poignant tale of youthful beauty, loss, and the enduring power of love and grief. The annual search for Hylas by Heracles was commemorated by the inhabitants of Cius, who would call out his name in the hopes of finding him, a tradition that speaks to the lasting impact of the young hero's disappearance. Hylas's story also highlights the dangers of beauty and the allure of the natural world, represented by the seductive nymphs who ultimately claimed him.
subtitle: Lost to the nymphs' watery embrace
achievements: Subject of enduring grief for Heracles

keyMyths.title: Abduction by the Nymphs
keyMyths.description: Hylas, while fetching water, was lured into a spring by nymphs captivated by his beauty. He disappeared, never to be seen again, causing great distress to Heracles.

keyMyths.title: Heracles' Search
keyMyths.description: Heracles, overwhelmed by grief, searched tirelessly for Hylas. His frantic search delayed the Argonauts and ultimately led him to abandon the quest.

keyMyths.title: Annual Commemoration
keyMyths.description: The people of Cius annually searched for Hylas, calling out his name. This tradition honored Heracles' grief and the enduring impact of Hylas' disappearance.

sources.author: Apollonius Rhodius
sources.description: Primary source detailing Hylas' journey and disappearance.
sources.text: Primary source detailing Hylas' journey and disappearance.
